visa_types.overview:
Overview
Student visas are given to bona fide students to pursue regular studies at recognized institutions in India. The visa is multiple entries and valid for the period of study as approved by the educational institution in India.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Student visas are given to bona fide students to pursue regular studies at recognized institutions in India. The visa is multiple entries and valid for the period of study as approved by the educational institution in India.

## Visa Fees
Your payment allows you to submit your application through the Indian Visa Application Centre. Your application can be accepted, refused or modified by the Embassy of India in its full right. Once service charges have been paid, they cannot be refunded, even in the case of a visa refusal or cancelling an application.
Important:
If sending your application by post, please do not forget to enclose the courier service charge to have your passport sent back to you by secure courier. Please refer to the below Fee Charge Table for details. 

[ind > che > en > Table:Study Visa > Visa Fees 1]

- * All payments to be made in CHF (Swiss Francs) only
- * All above payments include Bank Charges CHF 3.75 and applicable VAT
PAYMENT INSTRUCTION 
- Mode of Payments
- Debit & Credit Cards (At Visa Centre): ( Post Card & American Express will not be accepted )
- Cash (At Visa Centre)
- Payment by Postal Order
Please make your payment in favour of "VF SERVICES (UK) Limited" Weststrasse 2, 3005 Bern via E-Banking IBAN (CH31 0023 0230 5583 7301 X) or You may generate the QR code by accessing the following link https://qrbill.ubs.com/app/CAL/ (https://qrbill.ubs.com/app/CAL/)
Refund  of Visa Application Fee
If  VFS is in breach of contract or negligent in relation to any visa application  submitted, resulting in the visa not being granted or loss of applicants  passport with valid visas, in each case, wherein the applicant has to apply for  a new passport or cannot travel to India at the times stated by the applicant  in his/her visa application, then VFS will refund to the applicant the amount  of the visa application fee only.
Loss  or damage to passports and other travel documents. 
VFS  takes every reasonable precaution while handling an applicant's passport &  documents, and if due to any breach of contract or negligence on the part of  VFS, the applicant's submitted passport or documents are lost or damaged beyond  a useable condition, then VFS will pay to the applicant the amount of the fees  charged by the government of the applicant's home country, for the replacement  of the lost or damaged passport or other documents through their normal  replacement procedure, and VFS will assist the applicant with the same within  its capacity. Such payments will be made by VFS to the applicant on the  presentation of the payment receipt issued by the government of their  respective home country for the replacement of such lost or damaged passport or  documents.

## Documents Required
- Visa application form duly filled and signed by the applicant.
- Two recent   colour  passport size (about 50mm x 50mm) photograph, showing full frontal view of face  against a white background to be pasted on the application form.
- Current Valid passport with minimum of 180 days.
- Requisite  fee
3rd Country nationals are required to submit a copy of valid residence permit B  or C.
- Letter of admission from  recognized educational institution with duration of the course 
A copy of your flight reservation details (Travel itinerary)
- Bona fide letter the school / college
- Receipt / Acknowledgement of fees paid to the school / college.
- Documents showing financial support for tuition fees and stay in India.
- Proof of English specking efficiency (Preferably a high school level certificate of English subject or equivalent proof of English course studies
- Proof of present academic level of studies
- Proof of financial support preferably a bank statement
- All applicants  will be required to download and sign a Declaration Form, which will be made  available on the website
http://in.vfsglobal.ch/pdf/Declaration.pdf/ (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/u65BgPsutPvKh29vvdnyR/48cae01a69b7286f0b2f5cd267dbdc5b/Declaration.pdf)
and submit it  along with the application at the Visa  Centre

## Photo Specifications
It is imperative that the photograph submitted at the time of application must fall within the specifications and sizes given below.
Unacceptable photographs, that do not meet the specifications, may not be accepted for submission.
Sample Photograph:
- The photograph  should be in  colour  and of the size of 2 inch x 2 inch (50 mm x 50 mm).
- The photo-print should be clear and with a continuous-tone quality.
- It should have full face, front view, eyes open.
- Photo should present full head from top of hair to bottom of chin.
- Centre head within frame.
- The background  should be a plain light  colour background.
- There should not be any distracting shadows on the face or on the background.
- Head coverings are not permitted except for religious reasons, but the facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of the face must be clearly shown.
- The expression on the face should look natural.
Do’s and Don’ts for a Proper photograph
The photo for each applicant submitted must measure: 
- The photo should capture full face, front view, with eyes open.
- The head should be in the centre of the frame.
- There should be no distracting shadows on the face or background.
- Make sure photo presents full head from top of hair to bottom of chin; height of head should measure 1 inch to 1-3/8 inches (25 mm to 35 mm). Make sure eye height is between 1- 1/8 inches to 1-3.
Lighting on face and background 
- The light should be even and balanced to avoid shadows on the face.
- Background behind the face should be properly illuminated to avoid shadows in the background.
Photograph Print Properties 
- The photograph should be in size 2 inch x 2 inch (50 mm x 50 mm) and in color.
- Print photo on thin photo paper.
- Ensure the print is clear and has a continuous-tone quality.
- Do not retouch or otherwise enhance or soften the photo.
Resolution and Printing Quality/Contrast and  colour
- High-resolution photography and printing are strongly recommended.
- Both conventional and digital photography are acceptable, and conventional or digital printing methods may be used.
- Resulting print should exhibit a continuous-tone quality regardless of the print method used (dye sublimation, ink jet, laser, etc.)
- Digitally printed photos should be produced without visible pixels or dot patterns
- Fine facial features should be discernible.
- Brightness and contrast should be adjusted to present the subject and background accurately.
- Photos without  proper contrast or  colour  may obscure unique facial features.
- colour should reproduce natural skin tones.
- Fluorescent or other lighting with
- Unbalanced colour may cause unwanted  colour  cast in the photo. Appropriate filters can eliminate improper  colour  balance.
Head & Eyes- Position and Background
- Head should face the camera directly and should not tilt or turned (portrait style).
- Photo must show both edges of the face clearly.
- The eyes must be open, level and clearly visible and must not be covered by hairs or eyeglass frames.
Eyeglasses
- Glare on eyeglasses should be avoided with a slight upward or downward tilt of the head.
- No tinted or dark glasses.
Head coverings are not permitted except for religious reasons, but the facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of the face must be clearly shown. 
Exposure and Lighting
- There should not be any over-exposure or underexposure which results in an unusable photo.
- There should be a three-point balanced lighting. Facial features should be clearly evident in the photo.
- Lighting should be adjusted to avoid shadows on the face or background. Diffuse sources of light, such as umbrella lights, are preferable to point sources.
Flexibility in Children’s photographs
- Babies under one year do not have to have their eyes open.
- As regards the photograph of the children under ten years of age, requirements can be somewhat relaxed in respect of height of the face and the position of the eyes in the photographs; and in case of babies and infants also, the position of the face, the facial expression, the eyes and the line of sight can have some relaxation. However, a frontal photograph with clarity is required for children.
- It should show the child alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible) looking at the camera with a neutral expression and the mouth closed.

## Processing Time
Average processing times vary as explained below. Please finalize your travel arrangements only after you obtain an appropriate visa. VFS / Embassy of India, Bern will not be responsible for any inconvenience/problems faced as a result of finalizing travel plans before obtaining your appropriate visa. 
Normal Applications 
Normal Tourist / Business  visas up to a period of 6 / 12 months can be issued within 5 - 7 working days for foreign nationals, but visa requested in exceptional cases for a duration of 1 year or more, will take a minimum of 7 - 10 working days .
Most Tourist / Business visas up to a period of 6 / 12 months for Swiss and Liechtenstein nationals would be processed within 2-3 working days after submission of application except as detailed below 
- Employment, research, student , Volunteers visa may take up to 3 weeks or more
- Processing time for the below mentioned nationalities will take a minimum 7- 10 working days.
- Nationals of Bangladesh or any applicant (irrespective of current nationality) who previously held Bangladeshi nationality
- Sri Lankan nationals or Swiss Nationals of Sri Lankan Origin
Please note that the above mentioned times are dependent on the Embassy of India, Bern and will not be same for all visa cases. 
Same Day Visa Service
Effective 01st April 2014 the Embassy of India will no longer  process same day (Urgent) visa applications.
Please note: The processing times for visas are  estimates only. We do not guarantee the visa will be issued same day. Non-Swiss  nationals and Swiss nationals who require a reference have a processing time of  7-10 working days. These are based on information and experience of the Embassy  of India, Bern.  
The processing of your application is subject to the procedures  and timescales of the Embassy of India, Bern, over which we have no control

”
- Travel Itinerary:
Flight Reservation, E-Ticket or travel plan.  Note: Although a confirmed booking is not necessary, a detailed itinerary showing arrival and departure dates as well as flight details is compulsory.  Please include one copy per application.
- Payment by Postal Order:
Please review our visa fees and make your payment in favor of "VF SERVICES (UK) Limited" Weststrasse 2, 3005 Bern via E-Banking IBAN ( CH31 0023 0230 5583 7301 X ) or You may generate the QR code by accessing the following link
https://qrbill.ubs.com/app/CAL/ (https://qrbill.ubs.com/app/CAL/)
. Attach the payment confirmation with your application for your records.
- All applications submitted at our application centre must be paid for at time of submission via
- Credit or Debit Card (Post Card & American Express will not be accepted)
- Cash
LIST OF ADDITIONAL DOCUMENTS
If the applicant is a minor (under 15 years of age):
- Visa Application Form must be signed by a parent or legal guardian. Photocopy of ID or passport from parents or legal guardian as well as an authorization letter signed by the parents or legal guardian.
- Copy of Child’s Birth certificate.
If requesting double or multiple entry: Flight reservation showing visit to neighbouring countries or 2nd reservation for later date if intending to travel to India again within the validity of your visa. NOTE: This does not guarantee a multiple entry visa will be granted.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A Tourist Visa is given to those visiting India for tourism or other non-business (study, employment, etc...) related purposes. Valid for single entry for the period the return ticket has been booked with a maximum limit of 3 months. Irrespective of the duration of validity of visa, on each visit maximum period of stay in India is limited to 90 days, is non-extendable and change of purpose is not allowed. The processing time for tourist visas is 3-5 working days.
Bearing this in mind we strongly recommend applying close to the travel date. The number of entries is generally limited to single entry. However exceptions are made for those who travel to Nepal or Bhutan based on the travel itinerary. This has to be flawlessly documented and clearly indicated at the point of submission of the application.
Tourist visa for up to 5 years are available under the following condition: The applicant must be able to provide proof of travel to India 3-4 times a year spanning the last 3 years or more.
Tourist Visa

- Babies under one year do not have to have their eyes open.

Visas for international conferences, or on certain subjects, as decided by the Government of India from time to time, can be issued only after clearance for the said conference is received from the Government of India. Conference visa is issued for duration of 3 months with single entry.

## Visa Fees
Your payment allows you to submit your application through the  Indian Visa Application Centre. Your application can be accepted, refused or  modified by the Embassy of India in its full right. Once service charges have been  paid, they cannot be refunded, even in the case of a visa refusal or cancelling  an application.

- The expression on the face should look natural.

- The head should be in the centre of the frame.
- There should be no distracting shadows on the face or background.
- Make sure photo presents full head from top of hair to bottom of chin; height of head should measure 1 inch to 1-3/8 inches (25 mm to 35 mm). Make sure eye height is between 1- 1/8 inches to 1-3.
Lighting on face and background 
- The light should be even and balanced to avoid shadows on the face.
- Background behind the face should be properly illuminated to avoid shadows in the background.

- The photograph  should be in size 2 inch x 2 inch (50 mm x 50 mm) and in  colour.
- Print photo on thin photo paper.
- Ensure the print is clear and has a continuous-tone quality.
- Do not retouch or otherwise enhance or soften the photo.

- Fine facial features should be discernible.
- Brightness and contrast should be adjusted to present the subject and background accurately.
- Photos without proper contrast or colour may obscure unique facial features.
- colour should reproduce natural skin tones.

- Unbalanced colour may cause unwanted colour cast in the photo. Appropriate filters can eliminate improper colour balance.

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A Long term visa (5 yrs. Visa) issued by the Embassy of India for the Non-Business related purposes. For transferring a long term visa from an old passport to a new passport, applicants are required to submit the old passport which holds the valid India Visa and the current valid passport. To transfer a long term visa, it should a remaining validity of at least six month (180 days), on the day you submit your application. Please note that your visa can only be transferred from the respective Embassy of India, where it was issued originally.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
Given to professional journalists and photographers for up to three month's stay in India. If you are intending to make a documentary in India, kindly contact the Press and Information wing in the Embassy.

## Visa Fees
Your payment allows you to submit your application through the  Indian Visa Application Centre. Your application can be accepted, refused or  modified by the Embassy of India in its full right. Once service charges have been  paid, they cannot be refunded, even in the case of a visa refusal or cancelling  an application.
Important: 
If sending your application by post, please do not forget to enclose the courier service charge to have your passport sent back to you by secure courier. Please refer to the below Fee Charge Table for details.

[ind > che > en > Table:Journalist Visa > Visa Fees 1]
*All payments to be made in CHF (Swiss Francs) only
* Please find below transactions fees applicable on various modes of payments, including applicable VAT:
- Credit Cards: 3% on the total invoice amount
- Debit Card: CHF 0.30 per transaction
- Cash: No transaction fee applicable
- Payment by Postal Order: CHF 3.75 per transaction
PAYMENT INSTRUCTION 
- Urgent Visa (Same Day Visa) will not be issued for Journalist Visa
- Mode of Payments
- Debit & Credit Cards (At Visa Centre): ( Post Card & American Express will not be accepted )
- Cash (At Visa Centre)
- Payment by Postal Order
Please make your payment in favour of "VF SERVICES (UK) Limited" Weststrasse 2, 3005 Bern via E-Banking IBAN (CH31 0023 0230 5583 7301 X) or You may generate the QR code by accessing the following link https://qrbill.ubs.com/app/CAL/ (https://qrbill.ubs.com/app/CAL/)

## Documents Required
- Visa application form duly filled and signed by the applicant.
- Two recent colour passport size (about 50 mm x 50mm) photograph, showing full frontal view of face against a white background to be pasted on the application form.
- Current Valid passport with minimum of 180 days.
A copy of your flight reservation details (Travel itinerary)
- 3rd Country nationals are required to submit a copy of valid residence permit B or C
- A letter of declaration for all Audio / Visual equipment the applicant wants to carry to India.
- All applicants  will be required to download and sign a Declaration Form, which will be made  available on the website
http://in.vfsglobal.ch/pdf/Declaration.pdf/ (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/u65BgPsutPvKh29vvdnyR/48cae01a69b7286f0b2f5cd267dbdc5b/Declaration.pdf)
and submit it along with the application at the Visa  Centre

## Photo Specifications
It is imperative that the photograph submitted at the time of application must fall within the specifications and sizes given below.
Unacceptable photographs, that do not meet the specifications, may not be accepted for submission.
Sample Photograph:
- The photograph  should be in  colour  and of the size of 2 inch x 2 inch (50 mm x 50 mm).
- The photo-print should be clear and with a continuous-tone quality.
- It should  have full face, front  view;  eyes  open. Photo  should present full head from top of hair to bottom of chin.
- Centre head within frame.
- The background should be a plain light colour background.
- There should not be any distracting shadows on the face or on the background.
- Head coverings are not permitted except for religious reasons, but the facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of the face must be clearly shown.
- The expression on the face should look natural.
Do’s and Don’ts for a Proper photograph
The photo for each applicant submitted must measure: 
- The photo should capture full face, front view, with eyes open.
- The head should be in the centre of the frame.
- There should be no distracting shadows on the face or background.
- Make sure photo presents full head from top of hair to bottom of chin; height of head should measure 1 inch to 1-3/8 inches (25 mm to 35 mm). Make sure eye height is between 1- 1/8 inches to 1-3.
Lighting on face and background 
- The light should be even and balanced to avoid shadows on the face.
- Background behind the face should be properly illuminated to avoid shadows in the background.
Photograph Print Properties 
- The photograph  should be in size 2 inch x 2 inch (50 mm x 50 mm) and in  colour.
- Print photo on thin photo paper.
- Ensure the print is clear and has a continuous-tone quality.
- Do not retouch or otherwise enhance or soften the photo.
Resolution and Printing Quality/Contrast and  colour
- High-resolution photography and printing are strongly recommended.
- Both conventional and digital photography are acceptable, and conventional or digital printing methods may be used.
- Resulting print should exhibit a continuous-tone quality regardless of the print method used (dye sublimation, ink jet, laser, etc.)
- Digitally printed photos should be produced without visible pixels or dot patterns
- Fine facial features should be discernible.
- Brightness and contrast should be adjusted to present the subject and background accurately.
- Photos without  proper contrast or  colour  may obscure unique facial features.
- colour should reproduce natural skin tones.
- Fluorescent or other lighting with
- Unbalanced colour may cause unwanted colour cast in the photo. Appropriate filters can eliminate improper colour balance.
Head & Eyes- Position and Background
- Head should face the camera directly and should not tilt or turned (portrait style).
- Photo must show both edges of the face clearly.
- The eyes must be open, level and clearly visible and must not be covered by hairs or eyeglass frames.
Eyeglasses
- Glare on eyeglasses should be avoided with a slight upward or downward tilt of the head.
- No tinted or dark glasses.
Head coverings are not permitted except for religious reasons, but the facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of the face must be clearly shown. 
Exposure and Lighting
- There should not be any over-exposure or underexposure which results in an unusable photo.
- There should be a three-point balanced lighting. Facial features should be clearly evident in the photo.
- Lighting should be adjusted to avoid shadows on the face or background. Diffuse sources of light, such as umbrella lights, are preferable to point sources.
Flexibility in Children’s photographs
- Babies under one year do not have to have their eyes open.
- As regards the photograph of the children under ten years of age, requirements can be somewhat relaxed in respect of height of the face and the position of the eyes in the photographs; and in case of babies and infants also, the position of the face, the facial expression, the eyes and the line of sight can have some relaxation. However, a frontal photograph with clarity is required for children.
- It should show the child alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible) looking at the camera with a neutral expression and the mouth closed.

## Processing Time
Average processing times vary as explained below. Please finalize your travel arrangements only after you obtain an appropriate visa. VFS / Embassy of India, Bern will not be responsible for any inconvenience/problems faced as a result of finalizing travel plans before obtaining your appropriate visa. 
Normal Applications 
Normal Tourist / Business  visas up to a period of 6 / 12 months can be issued within 5 - 7 working days for foreign nationals, but visa requested in exceptional cases for a duration of 1 year or more, will take a minimum of 7 - 10 working days .
Most Tourist / Business visas up to a period of 6 / 12 months for Swiss and Liechtenstein nationals would be processed within 2-3 working days after submission of application except as detailed below 
- Journalist, Employment, research, student and Volunteers visa may take up to 5 weeks or more.
- Processing time for the below mentioned nationalities will take a minimum 7- 10 working days.
- Nationals of Bangladesh or any applicant (irrespective of current nationality) who previously held Bangladeshi nationality
- Sri Lankan nationals or Swiss Nationals of Sri Lankan Origin
Please note that the above mentioned times are dependent on the Embassy of India, Bern and will not be same for all visa cases. 
Same Day Visa Service
Effective 01st April 2014 the Embassy of India will no longer process same day (Urgent) visa applications.
Please note: The processing times for visas are estimates only. We do not guarantee the visa will be issued same day. Non-Swiss nationals and Swiss nationals who require a reference have a processing time of 7-10 working days. These are based on information and experience of the Embassy of India, Bern. 
The processing of your application is subject to the procedures and timescales of the Embassy of India, Bern, over which we have no control

visa_types.overview:
Overview
A transit visa is granted for the sole purpose of enabling the holder to travel through India to reach his/her ultimate destination. Change of purpose is not allowed. The visa will ordinarily be valid for a single/double journey and for entry within 15 days of date of issue. The Transit visa is valid for direct transit only for a maximum period of 3 days. For a stay in India beyond 3 days, an appropriate visa should be obtained.

visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
An Employment Visa is granted to those who are an employee of an Indian company. The Embassy may grant Employment visa valid for a year irrespective of the duration of the contract. Further extension up to 5 years could be obtained from MHA/FRRO in the concerned state in India. An Employment visa can be issued to third country nationals who hold a work permit/ resident permit of Switzerland older than 3 years.

## Visa Fees
Your payment allows you to submit your application through the  Indian Visa Application Centre. Your application can be accepted, refused or  modified by the Embassy of India in its full right. Once service charges have been  paid, they cannot be refunded, even in the case of a visa refusal or cancelling  an application.
Important: 
If sending your application by post, please do not forget to enclose the courier service charge to have your passport sent back to you by secure courier. Please refer to the below Fee Charge Table for details. 

[ind > che > en > Table:Employment Visa > Visa Fees 1]

*All payments to be made in CHF (Swiss Francs) only
* Please find below transactions fees applicable on various modes of payments, including applicable VAT:
- Credit Cards: 3% on the total invoice amount
- Debit Card: CHF 0.30 per transaction
- Cash: No transaction fee applicable
- Payment by Postal Order: CHF 3.75 per transaction
PAYMENT INSTRUCTION 
Urgent  Visa (Same Day Visa) will not be issued for Employment Visa

## Documents Required
- Visa application form duly filled and signed by the applicant.
- Two recent color passport size (about 50 mm x 50mm) photograph, showing full frontal view of face against a white background to be pasted on the application form.
- Current Valid passport with minimum of 180 days.
- Requisite  fee
3rd Country nationals are required to submit a copy of valid residence permit B  or C.
Proof of Registration of the Company in India
Copies of academic certificates
A copy of your flight reservation details (Travel itinerary)
- Employment Appointment letter
- Contract Agreement indicating terms and conditions and including information on various allowances and period of contract.
- All applicants  will be required to download and sign a Declaration Form, which will be made  available on the website
http://in.vfsglobal.ch/pdf/Declaration.pdf/ (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/u65BgPsutPvKh29vvdnyR/48cae01a69b7286f0b2f5cd267dbdc5b/Declaration.pdf)
and submit it along with the application at the Visa  Centre

## Photo Specifications
It is imperative that the photograph submitted at the time of application must fall within the specifications and sizes given below.
Unacceptable photographs, that do not meet the specifications, may not be accepted for submission.
Sample Photograph:
- The photograph  should be in  colour  and of the size of 3 inch x 3 inch (50 mm x 50 mm).
- The photo-print should be clear and with a continuous-tone quality.
- It should have full face, front view, eyes open.
- Photo should present full head from top of hair to bottom of chin.
- Centre head within frame.
- The background  should be a plain light  colour background.
- There should not be any distracting shadows on the face or on the background.
- Head coverings are not permitted except for religious reasons, but the facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of the face must be clearly shown.
- The expression on the face should look natural.
Do’s and Don’ts for a Proper photograph
The photo for each applicant submitted must measure: 
- The photo should capture full face, front view, with eyes open.
- The head should be in the centre of the frame.
- There should be no distracting shadows on the face or background.
- Make sure photo presents full head from top of hair to bottom of chin; height of head should measure 1 inch to 1-3/8 inches (25 mm to 25 mm). Make sure eye height is between 1- 1/8 inches to 1-3.
Lighting on face and background 
- The light should be even and balanced to avoid shadows on the face.
- Background behind the face should be properly illuminated to avoid shadows in the background.
Photograph Print Properties 
- The photograph should be in size 3 inch x 3 inch (50 mm x 50 mm) and in color.
- Print photo on thin photo paper.
- Ensure the print is clear and has a continuous-tone quality.
- Do not retouch or otherwise enhance or soften the photo.
Resolution and Printing Quality/Contrast and  colour
- High-resolution photography and printing are strongly recommended.
- Both conventional and digital photography are acceptable, and conventional or digital printing methods may be used.
- Resulting print should exhibit a continuous-tone quality regardless of the print method used (dye sublimation, ink jet, laser, etc.)
- Digitally printed photos should be produced without visible pixels or dot patterns
- Fine facial features should be discernible.
- Brightness and contrast should be adjusted to present the subject and background accurately.
- Photos without  proper contrast or  colour  may obscure unique facial features.
- colour should reproduce natural skin tones.
- Fluorescent or other lighting with
- Unbalanced colour may cause unwanted  colour  cast in the photo. Appropriate filters can eliminate improper  colour  balance.
Head & Eyes- Position and Background
- Head should face the camera directly and should not tilt or turned (portrait style).
- Photo must show both edges of the face clearly.
- The eyes must be open, level and clearly visible and must not be covered by hairs or eyeglass frames.
Eyeglasses
- Glare on eyeglasses should be avoided with a slight upward or downward tilt of the head.
- No tinted or dark glasses.
Head coverings are not permitted except for religious reasons, but the facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of the face must be clearly shown. 
Exposure and Lighting
- There should not be any over-exposure or underexposure which results in an unusable photo.
- There should be a three-point balanced lighting. Facial features should be clearly evident in the photo.
- Lighting should be adjusted to avoid shadows on the face or background. Diffuse sources of light, such as umbrella lights, are preferable to point sources.
Flexibility in Children’s photographs
- Babies under one year do not have to have their eyes open.
- As regards the photograph of the children under ten years of age, requirements can be somewhat relaxed in respect of height of the face and the position of the eyes in the photographs; and in case of babies and infants also, the position of the face, the facial expression, the eyes and the line of sight can have some relaxation. However, a frontal photograph with clarity is required for children.
- It should show the child alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible) looking at the camera with a neutral expression and the mouth closed.

## Processing Time
Average processing times vary as explained below. Please finalize your travel arrangements only after you obtain an appropriate visa. VFS / Embassy of India, Bern will not be responsible for any inconvenience/problems faced as a result of finalizing travel plans before obtaining your appropriate visa. 
Normal Applications 
Normal Tourist / Business  visas up to a period of 6 / 13 months can be issued within 5 - 7 working days for foreign nationals, but visa requested in exceptional cases for a duration of 1 year or more, will take a minimum of 7 - 10 working days .
Most Tourist / Business visas up to a period of 6 / 13 months for Swiss and Liechtenstein nationals would be processed within 3-3 working days after submission of application except as detailed below 
- Employment, research, student , Volunteers visa may take up to 3 weeks or more
- Processing time for the below mentioned nationalities will take a minimum 7- 10 working days.
- Nationals of Bangladesh or any applicant (irrespective of current nationality) who previously held Bangladeshi nationality
- Sri Lankan nationals or Swiss Nationals of Sri Lankan Origin
Please note that the above mentioned times are dependent on the Embassy of India, Bern and will not be same for all visa cases. 
Same Day Visa Service
Effective 01st April 3014 the Embassy of India will no longer  process same day (Urgent) visa applications.
Please note: The processing times for visas are  estimates only. We do not guarantee the visa will be issued same day. Non-Swiss  nationals and Swiss nationals who require a reference have a processing time of  7-10 working days. These are based on information and experience of the Embassy  of India, Bern.  
The processing of your application is subject to the procedures  and timescales of the Embassy of India, Bern, over which we have no control

Overview

MEDICAL VISA FOR IVF -SURROGACY TREATMENT

Entering into surrogacy arrangement under any other visa not sought for surrogacy is punishable under the Indian law.
visa_types.full_text:
## Overview
A Medical visa is given to those who are traveling to India for medical treatment. The visa can be single/multiple entry. Please note that relevant letters/documents explaining details of the treatment, signed and stamped by the concerned hospitals/doctors are required to validate the purpose of travel. 
MEDICAL VISA FOR IVF -SURROGACY TREATMENT 
Any person seeking a Visa to India for purpose of entering into a surrogacy arrangement must ascertain beforehand whether the law of that country permits surrogacy and will provide appropriate travel documents to the child born out of surrogacy to accompany the commissioning parents back to their country.
Entering into surrogacy arrangement under any other visa not sought for surrogacy is punishable under the Indian law.

## Visa Fees
Your payment allows you to submit your application through the  Indian Visa Application Centre. Your application can be accepted, refused or  modified by the Embassy of India in its full right. Once service charges have been  paid, they cannot be refunded, even in the case of a visa refusal or cancelling  an application.
Important: 
If sending your application by post, please do not forget to enclose the courier service charge to have your passport sent back to you by secure courier. Please refer to the below Fee Charge Table for details. 

[ind > che > en > Table:Medical Visa > Visa Fees 1]
*All payments to be made in CHF (Swiss Francs) only
* Please find below transactions fees applicable on various modes of payments, including applicable VAT:
- Credit Cards: 3% on the total invoice amount
- Debit Card: CHF 0.30 per transaction
- Cash: No transaction fee applicable
- Payment by Postal Order: CHF 3.75 per transaction
PAYMENT INSTRUCTION 
Urgent Visa (Same Day Visa) will not be issued for Medical Visa 
- Mode of Payments
- Debit & Credit Cards (At Visa Centre): ( Post Card & American Express will not be accepted )
- Cash (At Visa Centre)
- Payment by Postal Order
Please make your payment in favour of "VF SERVICES (UK) Limited" Weststrasse 2, 3005 Bern via E-Banking IBAN (CH31 0023 0230 5583 7301 X) or You may generate the QR code by accessing the following link https://qrbill.ubs.com/app/CAL/ (https://qrbill.ubs.com/app/CAL/)

## Documents Required
- Visa application form duly filled and signed by the applicant.
- Two recent colour passport size (about 50 mm x 50mm) photograph, showing full frontal view of face against a white background to be pasted on the application form.
- Current Valid passport with minimum of 180 days.
A copy of your flight reservation details (Travel itinerary)
- 3rd Country nationals are required to submit a copy of valid residence permit B or C
- All applicants  will be required to download and sign a Declaration Form, which will be made  available on the website
http://in.vfsglobal.ch/pdf/Declaration.pdf/ (https://assets.ctfassets.net/xxg4p8gt3sg6/u65BgPsutPvKh29vvdnyR/48cae01a69b7286f0b2f5cd267dbdc5b/Declaration.pdf)
and submit it along with the application at the  Visa  Centre

## Photo Specifications
It is imperative that the photograph submitted at the time of application must fall within the specifications and sizes given below.
Unacceptable photographs, that do not meet the specifications, may not be accepted for submission.
Sample Photograph:
- The photograph  should be in  colour  and of the size of 2 inch x 2 inch (50 mm x 50 mm).
- The photo-print should be clear and with a continuous-tone quality.
- It should  have full face, front  view;  eyes  open. Photo  should present full head from top of hair to bottom of chin.
- Center head within frame.
- The background should be a plain light colored background.
- There should not be any distracting shadows on the face or on the background.
- Head coverings are not permitted except for religious reasons, but the facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of the face must be clearly shown.
- The expression on the face should look natural.
Do’s and Don’ts for a Proper photograph
The photo for each applicant submitted must measure: 
- The photo should capture full face, front view, with eyes open.
- The head should be in the centre of the frame.
- There should be no distracting shadows on the face or background.
- Make sure photo presents full head from top of hair to bottom of chin; height of head should measure 1 inch to 1-3/8 inches (25 mm to 35 mm). Make sure eye height is between 1- 1/8 inches to 1-3.
Lighting on face and background 
- The light should be even and balanced to avoid shadows on the face.
- Background behind the face should be properly illuminated to avoid shadows in the background.
Photograph Print Properties 
- The photograph  should be in size 2 inch x 2 inch (50 mm x 50 mm) and in  colour.
- Print photo on thin photo paper.
- Ensure the print is clear and has a continuous-tone quality.
- Do not retouch or otherwise enhance or soften the photo.
Resolution and Printing Quality/Contrast and  Colour
- High-resolution photography and printing are strongly recommended.
- Both conventional and digital photography are acceptable, and conventional or digital printing methods may be used.
- Resulting print should exhibit a continuous-tone quality regardless of the print method used (dye sublimation, ink jet, laser, etc.)
- Digitally printed photos should be produced without visible pixels or dot patterns
- Fine facial features should be discernible.
- Brightness and contrast should be adjusted to present the subject and background accurately.
- Photos without proper contrast or color may obscure unique facial features.
- Color should reproduce natural skin tones.
- Fluorescent or other lighting with
- Unbalanced color may cause unwanted color cast in the photo. Appropriate filters can eliminate improper color balance.
Head & Eyes- Position and Background
- Head should face the camera directly and should not tilt or turned (portrait style).
- Photo must show both edges of the face clearly.
- The eyes must be open, level and clearly visible and must not be covered by hairs or eyeglass frames.
Eyeglasses
- Glare on eyeglasses should be avoided with a slight upward or downward tilt of the head.
- No tinted or dark glasses.
Head coverings are not permitted except for religious reasons, but the facial features from bottom of chin to top of forehead and both edges of the face must be clearly shown. 
Exposure and Lighting
- There should not be any over-exposure or underexposure which results in an unusable photo.
- There should be a three-point balanced lighting. Facial features should be clearly evident in the photo.
- Lighting should be adjusted to avoid shadows on the face or background. Diffuse sources of light, such as umbrella lights, are preferable to point sources.
Flexibility in Children’s photographs
- Babies under one year do not have to have their eyes open.
- As regards the photograph of the children under ten years of age, requirements can be somewhat relaxed in respect of height of the face and the position of the eyes in the photographs; and in case of babies and infants also, the position of the face, the facial expression, the eyes and the line of sight can have some relaxation. However, a frontal photograph with clarity is required for children.
- It should show the child alone (no chair backs, toys or other people visible) looking at the camera with a neutral expression and the mouth closed.

## Processing Time
Average processing times vary as explained below. Please finalize your travel arrangements only after you obtain an appropriate visa. VFS / Embassy of India, Bern will not be responsible for any inconvenience/problems faced as a result of finalizing travel plans before obtaining your appropriate visa. 
Normal Applications 
Normal Tourist / Business  visas up to a period of 6 / 12 months can be issued within 5 - 7 working days for foreign nationals, but visa requested in exceptional cases for a duration of 1 year or more, will take a minimum of 7 - 10 working days .
Most Tourist / Business visas up to a period of 6 / 12 months for Swiss and Liechtenstein nationals would be processed within 2-3 working days after submission of application except as detailed below 
- Employment, research, student , Volunteers visa may take up to 3 weeks or more
- Processing time for the below mentioned nationalities will take a minimum 7- 10 working days.
- Nationals of Bangladesh or any applicant (irrespective of current nationality) who previously held Bangladeshi nationality
- Sri Lankan nationals or Swiss Nationals of Sri Lankan Origin
Please note that the above mentioned times are dependent on the Embassy of India, Bern and will not be same for all visa cases. 
Same Day Visa Service
Effective 01st April 2014 the Embassy of India will no longer process same day (Urgent) visa applications.
Please note: The processing times for visas are estimates only. We do not guarantee the visa will be issued same day. Non-Swiss nationals and Swiss nationals who require a reference have a processing time of 7-10 working days. These are based on information and experience of the Embassy of India, Bern. 
The processing of your application is subject to the procedures and timescales of the Embassy of India, Bern, over which we have no control

visa_types.overview:
Overview
An Entry Visa is given to those of India origin visiting India for tourism or other non-business related purposes. Members of the family of a person employed in India are also eligible for Entry visa. In the latter case, documents establishing the employment of the spouse along with a copy of his/her Employment visa must be attached. Valid for six months to five years stay with multiple entry. Please note the visa is valid beginning on the day it is issued. So a 6 Month visa issued on January 1 would be valid until June 30.
